does: This paper introduces the LLM ATT&CK Navigator, a framework that systematically maps attack techniques and tactics that leverage large language models in cybersecurity contexts. The work catalogs AI-enabled cyber threats by adapting the MITRE ATT&CK framework to identify and classify how LLMs can be weaponized or exploited across attack phases including reconnaissance, weaponization, delivery, exploitation, installation, command and control, actions on objectives, and exfiltration. The research documents concrete threat patterns, attack vectors, and vulnerabilities specific to LLM deployment, providing operational red team insights into the novel attack surface created by AI systems.
connects: This work builds on the established MITRE ATT&CK framework, which has long served as the standard taxonomy for adversarial techniques in cybersecurity. It extends prior adversarial AI research examining model robustness and prompt injection vulnerabilities by situating these technical concerns within an operationalized threat modeling context. The paper responds to growing literature on AI safety and security risks by providing a practical mapping of how emerging LLM capabilities create new attack chains and threat scenarios, complementing theoretical work on AI alignment and trustworthiness with concrete tactical and operational intelligence about abuse vectors.
enables: This framework enables defenders and security teams to conduct systematic threat assessments for LLM-integrated systems and develop appropriate mitigations tailored to AI-specific attack patterns. It establishes a standardized language for discussing AI-enabled threats across organizations, supporting the development of detection rules, defense strategies, and incident response procedures. The work points toward integrated security tooling that accounts for LLM risks alongside traditional cyber threats, supports security validation of AI systems, and provides a foundation for understanding how adversaries will likely evolve tactics as LLM capabilities advance and deployment scales across critical infrastructure.
